lead.title: Στην Κομισιόν η έκρηξη στον Ασπρόπυργο: «Ένα ακόμα προμήνυμα βιομηχανικού ατυχήματος μεγάλης έκτασης»

items.title: Γιάννης Μπουλέκος: Το μήνυμα μετά την συνεδρίαση της Κ.Ε. – «Ο ΣΥΡΙΖΑ είναι παρών»

lead.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Vows Revenge as Iran’s Top Diplomat Is in Oman For Talks

items.title: Man Arrested in Killing of U.K. Ex-Lawmaker Is Released
items.snippet: The police said the 26-year-old was no longer part of the investigation into the death of Ann Widdecombe and urged anyone with information about the crime to come forward.

items.title: England and Norway Resurrect a Rivalry for the World Cup
items.snippet: Along with historic and diplomatic ties, England’s club soccer teams have loyal fans in Norway. On Saturday, supporters will have to pick a side.

items.title: A Wildfire Killed 12 People in Southern Spain. Here’s the Latest.
items.snippet: Officials said more than 20 people were still missing because of the fire, one of the deadliest in Spain’s history. Most of those confirmed to have been killed were from Belgium or Britain.

items.title: ‘They Don’t Need People’: The Workers Left Behind by China’s Robot Drive
items.snippet: For out-of-work factory workers in Kunshan, a region made rich by electronics manufacturing, a park is the only place to go.

items.title: U.S. Citizen Tests Positive for Ebola in Democratic Republic of Congo
items.snippet: The American, who works for the humanitarian organization Samaritan’s Purse, is the second to be infected in an outbreak that continues to spread.

items.title: Inside the Canadian Lab Burning Down Buildings to Save Them
items.snippet: At the national fire safety facility in Ottawa, scientists and engineers are increasingly focused on the threat from the forest.

items.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Remains Absent, a Void at the Top of the Regime
items.snippet: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei did not appear at his father’s funeral this week, fueling speculation about his physical condition and leaving a power vacuum in a divided country.

items.title: At Least 12 People Die in Southern Spain Wildfire
items.snippet: The blaze in Andalusia, one of the deadliest on record in the country, also left more than 20 people missing. Many of the victims were foreign tourists, the Spanish authorities said.

items.title: Israel Struck an Iranian Steel Facility. Was It a Valid Military Target?
items.snippet: During the war, Israel attacked Iran’s steel plants, saying they provided forces with revenue and the means to make weapons, but it also hurt the civilian economy.

items.title: After Venezuela Earthquakes, a Youth Baseball League Counts the Dead
items.snippet: Children who played for Venezuela’s youth baseball league, a symbol of national pride, have been left injured, orphaned or dead.

items.title: Moscow Still Has Art and Culture. Just Don’t Say ‘Ukraine.’
items.snippet: An eerie limbo prevails at small art shows, independent theaters and private political clubs, where the war is the elephant in the room.

items.title: Royal Rapprochement: Harry and Meghan Pay a Rare Visit to the King and Queen
items.snippet: Friday’s visit to King Charles and Queen Camilla signaled a détente in relations with Prince Harry and his wife, who stepped down from royal duties and moved to the United States in 2020.

items.title: Fighting Fires With Figures, These Experts Are Trying to Stop Europe From Burning
items.snippet: A team of engineers, foresters and scientists is helping the continent prepare for wildfires from a giant science park in Italy. Their arsenal includes satellites, weather models and expert analysis.

items.title: Videos Shed Light on Pursuit Before ICE Killing in Houston
items.snippet: Surveillance footage obtained by The Times shows ICE agents driving aggressively in unmarked vehicles, but the moment of the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo remains murky.

lead.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
lead.snippet: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

items.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
items.snippet: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

items.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
items.snippet: Independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs reported in May that a security researcher with cyber firm GitGuardian alerted him to reams of exposed passwords stored in a publicly accessible GitHub repository, which an employee of a CISA contractor had uploaded.

items.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
items.snippet: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

items.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
items.snippet: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

items.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
items.snippet: Schneider, who formerly served as the CEO of Automattic and is a partner at True Ventures, says he is "all in" on the unconventional social media platform.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
items.snippet: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

items.title: Filing: College app Fizz accuses VC of sharing confidential startup information with rival Sidechat
items.snippet: Fizz has expanded its lawsuit against rival Sidechat, alleging that a Maveron VC shared its confidential information obtained during a fundraising meeting with the competing startup.

items.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
items.snippet: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

items.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
items.snippet: HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

items.title: China is catching up to Elon Musk’s reusable rockets

items.title: Disney+ is considering a free streaming tier, report says
items.snippet: The launch of free content would allow Disney+ to better compete with free services like YouTube and Tubi, which are capturing a growing share of consumers’ viewing time.

items.title: Dumb Co dared me to trade my iPhone for a hacked flip phone
items.snippet: Dumb Co sells flip phones that sync to your smartphone, bridging the infinite connectivity of the iPhone and the unrealistic limitations of an early 2000s relic.

items.title: Oratomic raises $300M to build a viable quantum computer that needs only 20K qubits
items.snippet: The massive round was co-led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures.

items.title: Netflix could be planning ‘always-on’ live TV channels
items.snippet: Amid signs of slowing engagement, Netflix is reportedly considering launching "always-on" live channels, giving subscribers something to tune into 24/7.

lead.title: Whistle-Blowers Accuse Kennedy Center of Contracting Flaws Under Trump
